key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $1.20, up 11.1% YoY from $1.08
Operating income of $343.3 million, up 3.8% YoY
Share repurchases of $395.1 million (4.0 million shares) executed during the quarter
qoq_changes: Assets and liabilities from discontinued operations dropped to zero, indicating a completed divestiture or spin-off
Short-term borrowings increased to $619.5 million, while the $1.0 billion current portion of long-term debt was retired or refinanced
Transition services agreement income generated $23.8 million in the quarter
new_risks: SG&A expenses increased 14.8% YoY to $1.39 billion, outpacing revenue growth and compressing operating margins
Increased reliance on short-term borrowings ($619.5 million vs $0 at the end of the prior fiscal year)
management_tone: Neutral to bullish; the company is signaling confidence through aggressive share repurchases and a cleaned-up balance sheet post-discontinued operations, despite ongoing SG&A cost pressures.
analyst_note: Dollar Tree delivered a solid Q3 2025 with 9.4% top-line growth and a notable $395M in share buybacks. The complete removal of discontinued operations from the balance sheet marks a clean slate, allowing investors to focus on the core business, provided management can successfully rein in outpacing SG&A expenses.
